task.title: List durable tool invocation evidence
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/546 by discovering and replaying every durable tool invocation in deterministic ID order without a mutable index.
acceptance_criteria: Empty stores return an empty list and pending plus every terminal status are included.
Results are sorted by stable invocation ID and remain identical after reopening.
Unrelated streams are excluded and listing appends no events.
Malformed owning stream IDs, payloads, and histories fail closed through typed errors.

attempts.summary: Established RED for deterministic invocation discovery.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused durable invocation target failed because ToolInvocationStore had no list method and ToolInvocationStoreError had no InvalidStreamId variant.
attempts.patch: Kept tests for empty stores, ordering, every status, reopening, unrelated streams, no writes, malformed IDs, and replay failures before implementation.

attempts.summary: Implemented marker-based snapshot discovery and replay.
attempts.diagnostic: The store discovers streams from existing intent events in one consistent SQLite read snapshot, validates the owned stream prefix and external ID, reuses projection replay, and sorts by invocation ID.
attempts.patch: Added ToolInvocationStore::list, centralized the stream prefix, introduced typed InvalidStreamId handling, and documented deterministic audit behavior without a separate index.

attempts.summary: Verified and merged the bounded listing slice.
attempts.diagnostic: Eleven focused tests and pinned just verify passed. Independent pre-commit review found no blocker. Rust quality gate run 30037011978 passed. CodeRabbit's automatic review reported no actionable comments; a later manual resume returned only Review finished after review had paused.
attempts.patch: Pull request 547 squash-merged as 68e0f914b37be030e1ccd79949786771ab0cd674 and closed issue 546.

outcome.summary: Vela can deterministically enumerate all durable tool invocation projections from authoritative event evidence without maintaining a second mutable index.

verification.command: /run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test durable_tool_invocations # 11 passed
Independent pre-commit diff review # no blocking findings
GitHub Rust quality gate https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/actions/runs/30037011978 and CodeRabbit review at https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/pull/547

lessons: Existing event types can serve as discovery markers, avoiding a second index that could diverge from authoritative streams.
Discovery should replay through the same projection logic as point loads so invariants do not split.
Deterministic sorting belongs at the public query boundary rather than relying on database row order.
Owning stream identifiers must be validated instead of silently skipping malformed durable evidence.
